content: (2) A FME in its capacity as a portfolio manager may have the following categories as clients: (a) a person resident outside India; (b) a non-resident Indian; (c) a non-individual resident in India who is eligible under FEMA to invest funds offshore, to the extent of outward investment permitted;
summary: Section 73(2) defines eligible clients for a FME acting as a portfolio manager, including persons resident outside India, non-resident Indians, and non-individual residents in India eligible for offshore investment under FEMA.

metadata.contained_chapter: Chapter VI - Other Fund Management Activities

content: to the extent of outward investment permitted; and (d) an individual resident in India who is eligible under FEMA to invest funds offshore, to the extent allowed under the liberalised remittance scheme (LRS) of Reserve Bank of India
summary: Section 73(2)(d) permits individual residents in India to be clients of a FME portfolio manager, provided they are eligible to invest offshore under the Reserve Bank of India's Liberalised Remittance Scheme (LRS).

metadata.contained_chapter: Chapter VI - Other Fund Management Activities

content: (3) A FME operating as a portfolio manager in an IFSC shall be permitted to invest in securities and financial products in an IFSC, India or Foreign Jurisdiction: Provided that in case of a discretionary portfolio management service, it shall invest in the securities listed or to be listed or
summary: Section 73(3) permits FMEs operating in an IFSC to invest in securities and financial products across IFSC, India, or foreign jurisdictions, with specific requirements for discretionary portfolio management services.

metadata.contained_chapter: Chapter VI - Other Fund Management Activities

content: in the securities listed or to be listed or traded on the stock exchanges, money market instruments, units of investment schemes and other financial products as specified by the Authority from time to time.
summary: Section 73 defines the scope of financial instruments subject to regulation, including listed securities, money market instruments, and investment scheme units as specified by the Authority.

metadata.contained_chapter: Chapter VI - Other Fund Management Activities

content: (1) The FME shall provide a disclosure document to the client, prior to entering into a portfolio management agreement with the client. (2) The FME shall ensure that a copy of disclosure document is available on its website
summary: Section 74(1)-(2) mandates that a Financial Management Entity (FME) must provide a disclosure document to clients before executing a portfolio management agreement and maintain a copy on its website.

metadata.contained_chapter: Chapter VI - Other Fund Management Activities

content: (3) The disclosure document referred to in sub-regulation (1) shall inter-alia contain details pertaining to the services offered, risk factors, client representation, financial performance, performance of portfolio manager, auditor observations, nature of expenses, taxation, investor grievance
summary: Section 74(3) specifies that the disclosure document must include details on services, risk factors, client representation, financial performance, auditor observations, taxation, and investor grievance mechanisms.

metadata.contained_chapter: Chapter VI - Other Fund Management Activities

content: nature of expenses, taxation, investor grievance redressal mechanism and litigations by the regulatory authorities against the portfolio manager and its principal officers, directors/ partners/ designated partners and key managerial personnel.
summary: Section 74 continues to mandate the disclosure of expenses, taxation, investor grievance redressal mechanisms, and any regulatory litigation against the FME, its principal officers, directors, partners, or key managerial personnel.

metadata.contained_chapter: Chapter VI - Other Fund Management Activities

content: (1) A FME shall enter into a written agreement with the portfolio management client that clearly defines the inter se relationship and sets out their mutual rights, liabilities and obligations relating to management of portfolio including details pertaining to investment objectives, risk factors,
summary: Section 75(1) requires a written agreement between the FME and the client to define their relationship, mutual rights, liabilities, obligations, investment objectives, and risk factors.

metadata.contained_chapter: Chapter VI - Other Fund Management Activities

content: to investment objectives, risk factors, terms of fees, period of the contract, etc
summary: Section 75(1) further mandates that the written agreement between the FME and the client must explicitly detail the terms of fees and the duration of the contract.

metadata.contained_chapter: Chapter VI - Other Fund Management Activities

content: (2) Notwithstanding anything contained in the agreement between the FME and the client, the funds or securities can be withdrawn by the client before the maturity of the contract under the following circumstances, namely- (a) voluntary or compulsory termination of portfolio management services by
summary: Section 75(2) permits clients to withdraw funds or securities before contract maturity in cases of voluntary or compulsory termination of services by either party.

metadata.contained_chapter: Chapter VI - Other Fund Management Activities

content: termination of portfolio management services by the FME or the client; (b) suspension or cancellation of the certificate of registration of the FME by the Authority; or (c) bankruptcy or liquidation of the FME.
summary: Section 75(2) allows early withdrawal of funds or securities if the FME's certificate of registration is suspended or cancelled by the Authority, or in the event of the FME's bankruptcy or liquidation.

metadata.contained_chapter: Chapter VI - Other Fund Management Activities

content: (1) The FME shall periodically furnish a report to the portfolio management client in terms of the agreement with the client which shall inter-alia contain details relating to composition and value of the portfolio, transactions undertaken during the period of the report, beneficial interest
summary: Section 76(1) requires the FME to periodically provide clients with reports detailing portfolio composition, valuation, transactions, and beneficial interest received.

metadata.contained_chapter: Chapter VI - Other Fund Management Activities

content: the period of the report, beneficial interest received during the period of the report, expenses incurred in managing the portfolio and details of risk relating to the securities recommended by the portfolio manager for investment or disinvestment
summary: Section 76(1) mandates that periodic reports include expenses incurred in portfolio management and specific risk disclosures regarding securities recommended for investment or disinvestment.

metadata.contained_chapter: Chapter VI - Other Fund Management Activities

content: (2) The report referred to in sub-regulation (1) may be made available online with restricted access to each client.
summary: Section 76(2) permits the online publication of reports with restricted access provided to each client.

metadata.contained_chapter: Chapter VI - Other Fund Management Activities

content: (1) A FME shall not accept from the client, funds or securities worth less than USD 75,000 in case of a portfolio management agreement: Provided that the minimum investment threshold shall not apply to an accredited investor
summary: Section 77(1) mandates a minimum investment threshold of USD 75,000 for portfolio management agreements, excluding accredited investors.

metadata.contained_chapter: Chapter VI - Other Fund Management Activities

content: (2) The funds of a client availing portfolio management services (other than those availing only advisory services) may be maintained in- (a) a specific bank account of the FME in a Banking Unit; (b) a specific bank account of the client in a Banking Unit, a bank in India or a Foreign Jurisdiction;
summary: Section 77(2) specifies that client funds for portfolio management services must be held in designated bank accounts within a Banking Unit, India, or a foreign jurisdiction.

metadata.contained_chapter: Chapter VI - Other Fund Management Activities

content: Unit, a bank in India or a Foreign Jurisdiction; (c) a specific account of the client maintained with a regulated broker dealer in IFSC, or an equivalent entity in India or foreign jurisdiction; or (d) any other manner as may be specified by the Authority
summary: Section 77(2)(c)-(d) permits client funds to be held in accounts with regulated broker-dealers in IFSC, India, or foreign jurisdictions, or as otherwise specified by the Authority.

metadata.contained_chapter: Chapter VI - Other Fund Management Activities

content: Provided that when the funds are maintained in the specific bank account of a client, the FME operating as portfolio manager shall ensure that it is duly authorised to operate the said bank account either by itself or through a custodian and it shall provide the details of all such bank accounts
summary: When funds are held in a client's specific bank account, the FME must be authorized to operate the account directly or via a custodian and must disclose all such account details.

metadata.contained_chapter: Chapter VI - Other Fund Management Activities

content: provide the details of all such bank accounts including transactions carried out thereunder, to the Authority, whenever directed to do so: Provided further that when the funds of the clients are in a specific account maintained with a regulated broker dealer, the FME operating as portfolio manager
summary: FMEs must provide the Authority with details and transaction records for all client bank accounts upon request.

metadata.contained_chapter: Chapter VI - Other Fund Management Activities

content: dealer, the FME operating as portfolio manager shall ensure that - (i) adequate controls are in place to ensure requirement specified under sub-regulation (1) is complied with; (ii) it is duly authorised to operate the said account, and (iii) it shall provide the details of all such accounts
summary: For client accounts held with regulated broker-dealers, the FME must maintain adequate controls, obtain authorization to operate the account, and provide account details to the Authority.

metadata.contained_chapter: Chapter VI - Other Fund Management Activities

content: it shall provide the details of all such accounts including transactions carried out thereunder, to the Authority, whenever directed to do so
summary: FMEs are required to submit details and transaction records of client accounts held with regulated broker-dealers to the Authority whenever directed.

metadata.contained_chapter: Chapter VI - Other Fund Management Activities

content: (3) A FME shall segregate each portfolio management client’s holding in securities in separate accounts: Provided that this sub-regulation shall not apply if the investments of the clients are in jurisdictions permitting omnibus account structure
summary: Section 77(3) requires FMEs to segregate securities holdings for each portfolio management client, unless the jurisdiction permits an omnibus account structure.

metadata.contained_chapter: Chapter VI - Other Fund Management Activities

content: In such cases, FME shall ensure that the investment using omnibus structure is pursuant to prior consent of the clients; and adequate checks are in place to ensure that the clients’ securities are earmarked separately
summary: When using an omnibus structure, the FME must obtain prior client consent and implement adequate checks to ensure individual client securities are earmarked separately.

metadata.contained_chapter: Chapter VI - Other Fund Management Activities

content: (4) The funds received from the clients, investments or disinvestments, all the credits to the account of the client including interest, dividend, bonus or any other beneficial interest received on the investment and debit for expenses, if any, shall be properly reflected in the client’s accounts
summary: Section 77(4) mandates that all client funds, investments, disinvestments, credits, interest, dividends, bonuses, and debits for expenses must be accurately reflected in the client's accounts.

metadata.contained_chapter: Chapter VI - Other Fund Management Activities

content: (5) The FME shall act in a fiduciary capacity in respect of the client’s funds and securities, and shall not derive any direct or indirect benefit out of the client’s funds or securities. (6) The FME shall not borrow funds or securities on behalf of the client.
summary: Section 77(5) and (6) establish that the FME acts in a fiduciary capacity, prohibiting the derivation of direct or indirect benefits from client assets and forbidding the borrowing of funds or securities on the client's behalf.

metadata.contained_chapter: Chapter VI - Other Fund Management Activities

content: (1) The money or securities accepted by the FME shall be invested or managed in terms of the portfolio management agreement between the FME and the client
summary: Section 78(1) requires that all money or securities accepted by the FME be invested or managed strictly according to the terms of the portfolio management agreement.

metadata.contained_chapter: Chapter VI - Other Fund Management Activities

content: (2) The FME shall not use the portfolio of its clients for investment in derivatives, unless express consent has been obtained from its clients
summary: Section 78(2) prohibits the FME from investing client portfolios in derivatives without obtaining express consent from the client.

metadata.contained_chapter: Chapter VI - Other Fund Management Activities

content: (3) The FME shall not enter into any transaction for purchase or sale of any security which is periodically or ultimately settled otherwise than by actual delivery or transfer of security except the transactions in derivatives
summary: Section 78(3) mandates that all security transactions must be settled by actual delivery or transfer, with the sole exception of derivative transactions.

metadata.contained_chapter: Chapter VI - Other Fund Management Activities

content: (4) A FME shall, ordinarily purchase or sell securities separately for each portfolio management client: Provided that in the event of aggregation of purchases or sales for economy of scale, inter se allocation shall be done on a pro rata basis and at weighted average price of the ’day's
summary: Section 78(4) requires FMEs to execute trades separately for each client, though aggregation for economy of scale is permitted provided that inter se allocation is performed on a pro rata basis at the weighted average price.

metadata.contained_chapter: Chapter VI - Other Fund Management Activities

content: basis and at weighted average price of the ’day's transactions and the FME shall not keep any open position in respect of allocation of sales or purchases effected in a day
summary: Section 78(4) further stipulates that FMEs must not maintain open positions regarding the allocation of daily purchases or sales.

metadata.contained_chapter: Chapter VI - Other Fund Management Activities

content: (5) The FME shall segregate each client's funds and portfolio of securities and keep them separately from its own funds and securities and be responsible for safekeeping of clients' funds and securities
summary: Section 78(5) requires the FME to segregate client funds and securities from its own assets and holds the FME responsible for their safekeeping.

metadata.contained_chapter: Chapter VI - Other Fund Management Activities

content: (6) The FME shall not hold the securities belonging to the portfolio account in its own name on behalf of its clients either by virtue of contract with clients or otherwise: Provided that if the investments of the clients are in jurisdictions permitting omnibus account structure such restriction
summary: Section 78(6) prohibits the FME from holding client securities in its own name, except in jurisdictions where omnibus account structures are legally permitted.

metadata.contained_chapter: Chapter VI - Other Fund Management Activities

content: omnibus account structure such restriction shall not apply: Provided further that in case of investments made by the FME through omnibus account structure, it shall ensure that such investments are made subject to prior consent of the clients, and adequate checks are in place to ensure that the
summary: Section 78(6) provides that investments made via omnibus account structures require prior client consent and the implementation of adequate internal checks.

metadata.contained_chapter: Chapter VI - Other Fund Management Activities

content: adequate checks are in place to ensure that the clients’ securities are earmarked separately
summary: Section 78 mandates the implementation of adequate internal controls to ensure the distinct earmarking and segregation of client securities.

metadata.contained_chapter: Chapter VI - Other Fund Management Activities

content: (7) The FME operating as a portfolio manager, except those providing only advisory services, shall appoint a custodian in respect of securities managed or administered by it.
summary: Under Section 78(7), an FME acting as a portfolio manager, excluding those providing solely advisory services, is required to appoint a custodian for all managed or administered securities.

metadata.contained_chapter: Chapter VI - Other Fund Management Activities

content: (1) The FME shall charge an agreed fee from the clients for rendering portfolio management services without guaranteeing or assuring, either directly or indirectly, any return and the fee so charged may be a fixed fee or a return-based fee or a combination of both
summary: Section 79(1) permits FMEs to charge fixed, return-based, or hybrid fees for portfolio management services, provided that no direct or indirect guarantee of returns is offered to the client.

metadata.contained_chapter: Chapter VI - Other Fund Management Activities

content: (2) The FME in its capacity as a discretionary portfolio manager shall individually and independently manage the funds of the client in accordance with the needs of the client, in a manner which does not partake the character of a retail fund, whereas a non-discretionary portfolio manager shall
summary: Section 79(2) defines the discretionary portfolio manager's role as managing client funds independently and individually, ensuring the service does not function as a retail fund.

metadata.contained_chapter: Chapter VI - Other Fund Management Activities

content: a non-discretionary portfolio manager shall manage the funds of the client in accordance with the directions of the client
summary: Section 79(2) stipulates that a non-discretionary portfolio manager must manage client funds strictly in accordance with the specific directions provided by the client.

metadata.contained_chapter: Chapter VI - Other Fund Management Activities

content: (3) The FME shall ensure that any person or entity involved in the distribution of its services is carrying out the distribution activities in compliance with these regulations and the circulars and directions issued thereunder by the Authority from time to time
summary: Section 79(3) requires FMEs to ensure that all entities involved in the distribution of their services comply with applicable regulations, circulars, and Authority directives.

metadata.contained_chapter: Chapter VI - Other Fund Management Activities

content: (4) The FME shall report its performance uniformly in the disclosures to the Authority, advertising material, reports to the clients and on its website
summary: Section 79(4) mandates that FMEs maintain uniform performance reporting across all client reports, advertising materials, website disclosures, and submissions to the Authority.

metadata.contained_chapter: Chapter VI - Other Fund Management Activities

content: (5) The portfolio accounts managed and administered by the FME in its capacity as a portfolio manager shall be audited annually and a copy of the certificate shall be given to the client.
summary: Section 79(5) requires an annual audit of all portfolio accounts managed by the FME, with a mandatory certificate provided to the client.

metadata.contained_chapter: Chapter VI - Other Fund Management Activities

content: A FME as part of its portfolio management services shall enter into an agreement with prospective clients for providing advisory services, provided: (a) it complies with the Regulation 43 to 50 of the IFSCA (Capital Market Intermediaries) Regulations, 2021
summary: Section 80 requires FMEs providing advisory services to enter into formal client agreements and comply with Regulations 43 to 50 of the IFSCA (Capital Market Intermediaries) Regulations, 2021.

metadata.contained_chapter: Chapter VI - Other Fund Management Activities

content: (b) it complies with the code of conduct under the IFSCA (Capital Market Intermediaries) Regulations, 2021; and (c) Advisory services are for a portfolio not less than USD 75,000 in value.
summary: Section 80 mandates that FMEs providing advisory services adhere to the code of conduct under the IFSCA (Capital Market Intermediaries) Regulations, 2021, and maintain a minimum portfolio value of USD 75,000.

metadata.contained_chapter: Chapter VI - Other Fund Management Activities

content: (1) A FME may also provide services to multi-family office under a portfolio management agreement as detailed in this Chapter
summary: Section 81(1) authorizes a Fund Management Entity (FME) to provide services to a multi-family office under a portfolio management agreement.

metadata.contained_chapter: Chapter VI - Other Fund Management Activities

content: (2) The Authority may specify additional conditions, additional permissible investment, etc., for FMEs providing services to multi-family office under a portfolio management agreement.
summary: Section 81(2) empowers the Authority to impose additional conditions and specify permissible investments for FMEs operating under portfolio management agreements for multi-family offices.

metadata.contained_chapter: Chapter VI - Other Fund Management Activities

content: for Investment Trust whose units are listed or proposed to be listed on a recognised stock exchange(s)

content: (4) The Investment Trust shall comply with the requirements specified by the recognised stock exchange(s).
summary: Section 82(4) mandates that Investment Trusts must adhere to all requirements specified by the relevant recognised stock exchange(s).
