content: A registered FME shall have robust cyber security and cyber resilience framework in accordance with the requirements as may be specified by the Authority from time to time.
summary: Section 122 mandates that a registered FME implement a robust cyber security and cyber resilience framework as specified by the Authority.

metadata.contained_chapter: Chapter VIII - General Obligations and Responsibilities

content: (1) A FME shall have a sound risk management system for comprehensively managing all risks
summary: Section 123(1) requires an FME to maintain a sound risk management system for the comprehensive management of all risks.

metadata.contained_chapter: Chapter VIII - General Obligations and Responsibilities

content: (2) A FME shall have adequate internal procedures and controls, given the types of business in which it engages, including any activities which have been outsourced, with the aim of protecting the interests of clients/investors and their assets and ensuring proper management of risk.
summary: Section 123(2) requires an FME to establish adequate internal procedures and controls, including for outsourced activities, to protect client/investor assets and ensure effective risk management.

metadata.contained_chapter: Chapter VIII - General Obligations and Responsibilities

content: No guaranteed return shall be offered in a scheme or under an agreement for PMS, (a) unless such returns are fully guaranteed by the FME; (b) unless a statement indicating the guarantee including details thereof is made in the offer document / agreement; (c) the manner in which the guarantee is to
summary: Section 124 prohibits guaranteed returns in Portfolio Management Services (PMS) unless the FME fully guarantees the returns and discloses the guarantee details and fulfillment method in the offer document or agreement.

metadata.contained_chapter: Chapter VIII - General Obligations and Responsibilities

content: (c) the manner in which the guarantee is to be met has been stated in the offer document / agreement.
summary: Section 124(c) stipulates that the specific manner in which a guaranteed return is to be met must be explicitly stated in the offer document or agreement.

metadata.contained_chapter: Chapter VIII - General Obligations and Responsibilities

content: (1) A FME shall seek prior approval of the Authority in case of any direct or indirect change in control of the FME: Provided that where a FME operating in the form of branch is required to take prior approval from its sectoral regulator in its principal place of operation, it shall only inform
summary: Section 125(1) requires an FME to obtain prior approval from the Authority for any direct or indirect change in control, with an exception for branch-based FMEs regulated by a principal sectoral regulator.

metadata.contained_chapter: Chapter VIII - General Obligations and Responsibilities

content: place of operation, it shall only inform such change to the Authority, within fifteen (15) days thereof
summary: Under Section 125(1), an FME operating as a branch that is regulated by a principal sectoral regulator must notify the Authority of any change in control within fifteen days.

metadata.contained_chapter: Chapter VIII - General Obligations and Responsibilities

content: (2) The Authority may consider such request for change in control subject to such conditions, as deemed appropriate including offering exit opportunity to investors.
summary: Under Section 125(2), the Authority may approve a change in control for a Fund Management Entity (FME) subject to specific conditions, including the requirement to provide an exit opportunity to investors.

metadata.contained_chapter: Chapter VIII - General Obligations and Responsibilities

content: A FME shall pay the fees pertaining to annual fees, scheme filing fee or any other fees as may be specified by the Authority from time to time.
summary: Section 126 mandates that a Fund Management Entity (FME) must pay all applicable annual fees, scheme filing fees, and any other charges specified by the Authority.

metadata.contained_chapter: Chapter VIII - General Obligations and Responsibilities

content: Advertisements issued by FME, if any, shall be in conformity with the Advertisement Code as specified in the Fifth Schedule of these regulations.
summary: Section 127 requires that any advertisements issued by a Fund Management Entity (FME) must strictly comply with the Advertisement Code outlined in the Fifth Schedule of the regulations.

metadata.contained_chapter: Chapter VIII - General Obligations and Responsibilities

content: (1) All fees and expenses should be clearly identified and appropriated separately for each scheme
summary: Section 128(1) stipulates that all fees and expenses must be clearly identified and accounted for separately for each individual scheme.

metadata.contained_chapter: Chapter VIII - General Obligations and Responsibilities

content: (2) The FME shall ensure suitable disclosure in the offer document / placement memorandum regarding the maximum fees and expenses that it may charge and each expense item shall be disclosed separately as a specific line item in the offer document / placement memorandum.
summary: Section 128(2) requires the FME to disclose the maximum fees and expenses in the offer document or placement memorandum, with each expense item listed as a distinct line item.

metadata.contained_chapter: Chapter VIII - General Obligations and Responsibilities

content: (1) The FME may, at its discretion, constitute an Investment Committee to make investment decisions for the schemes
summary: Section 129(1) grants the FME the discretion to establish an Investment Committee responsible for making investment decisions for its schemes.

metadata.contained_chapter: Chapter VIII - General Obligations and Responsibilities

content: (2) All responsibilities casted upon the FME and Fund Managers under these regulations, to the extent applicable, shall also be complied with by the members of such Investment Committee.
summary: Section 129(2) mandates that members of the Investment Committee must comply with all regulatory responsibilities applicable to the FME and Fund Managers.

metadata.contained_chapter: Chapter VIII - General Obligations and Responsibilities

content: Merger, demerger and restructuring of the scheme(s) shall be in accordance with the conditions as may be specified by the Authority and with the prior approval by the Authority.
summary: Section 130 requires that any merger, demerger, or restructuring of schemes must receive prior approval from the Authority and adhere to its specified conditions.

metadata.contained_chapter: Chapter VIII - General Obligations and Responsibilities

content: (1) A scheme of the FME may be wound up:- (a) When the tenure of the scheme, as mentioned in the placement memorandum / offer document, is over; (b) If seventy-five per cent
summary: Section 131(1)(a) provides that a scheme may be wound up upon the expiration of its tenure as defined in the placement memorandum or offer document.

metadata.contained_chapter: Chapter VIII - General Obligations and Responsibilities

content: (75%) of the investors, by value of their investment in the scheme, pass a resolution at a meeting of investors that the scheme be wound up
summary: Section 131(1)(b) allows for the winding up of a scheme if investors representing at least seventy-five percent (75%) of the total investment value pass a resolution to that effect.

metadata.contained_chapter: Chapter VIII - General Obligations and Responsibilities

content: (c) If the FME has raised funds from the investors under the scheme but fails to achieve the minimum corpus during the validity or extended validity of the placement memorandum or offer document, as applicable, and the FME has not extended the validity thereof by making the requisite filing and
summary: Section 131(c) addresses the failure of an FME to reach the minimum corpus for a scheme within the validity period of the placement memorandum or offer document without filing for an extension.

metadata.contained_chapter: Chapter VIII - General Obligations and Responsibilities

content: thereof by making the requisite filing and payment of fee to the Authority
summary: This segment specifies the requirement for an FME to make a requisite filing and pay the prescribed fee to the Authority to extend the validity of a placement memorandum or offer document.

metadata.contained_chapter: Chapter VIII - General Obligations and Responsibilities

content: (d) When no investors have been onboarded into the scheme and no funds have been collected and the FME voluntarily desires to wind up the same
summary: Section 131(d) outlines the procedure for an FME to voluntarily wind up a scheme when no investors have been onboarded and no funds have been collected.

metadata.contained_chapter: Chapter VIII - General Obligations and Responsibilities

content: (2) The Authority, in the interest of investors and for orderly development of the financial market, may direct a FME to:- (a) wind up a scheme, subject to such conditions as deemed appropriate; (b) merge certain schemes; or (c) manage schemes of other FMEs.
summary: Section 131(2) empowers the Authority to direct an FME to wind up, merge, or manage schemes of other FMEs in the interest of investors and financial market development.

metadata.contained_chapter: Chapter VIII - General Obligations and Responsibilities

content: The FME shall appoint an independent custodian to provide the custodial services for the following schemes:- (a) Retail schemes; (b) Open ended restricted schemes; and (c) All other schemes managing AUM above USD 70 Million
summary: Section 132 mandates that FMEs appoint an independent custodian for retail schemes, open-ended restricted schemes, and any schemes with Assets Under Management (AUM) exceeding USD 70 million.

metadata.contained_chapter: Chapter VIII - General Obligations and Responsibilities

content: Provided that the requirement of appointment of custodian shall not be mandatory for fund of funds schemes where the underlying scheme(s) have appointed independent custodian(s). Explanation I
summary: The mandatory custodian requirement under Section 132 is waived for fund of funds schemes provided the underlying schemes have already appointed independent custodians.

metadata.contained_chapter: Chapter VIII - General Obligations and Responsibilities

content: Explanation I. – The Custodian appointed under this regulation shall be based in IFSC, unless the local laws of the jurisdiction where the securities have been issued mandate appointment of a custodian in that jurisdiction, in which case, the FME may appoint a custodian based in that jurisdiction
summary: Explanation I to Section 132 requires custodians to be based in the IFSC, unless local jurisdiction laws for issued securities mandate a custodian in that specific jurisdiction.

metadata.contained_chapter: Chapter VIII - General Obligations and Responsibilities

content: appoint a custodian based in that jurisdiction regulated by the financial sector regulator in that jurisdiction for such securities and make necessary arrangement to provide such information to Authority whenever directed to do so
summary: When a custodian is appointed in a foreign jurisdiction under Section 132, they must be regulated by the local financial sector regulator and provide information to the Authority upon request.

metadata.contained_chapter: Chapter VIII - General Obligations and Responsibilities

content: Explanation II
summary: Explanation II provides the regulatory framework for the timeline of custodian appointments under Section 132.

metadata.contained_chapter: Chapter VIII - General Obligations and Responsibilities

content: Explanation II. – In case of schemes which are required to appoint custodian in IFSC in terms of the abovementioned provision, such appointment may be made within twenty four (24) months from the date of commencement of the International Financial Services Centres Authority (Fund Management)
summary: Explanation II to Section 132 grants a 24-month grace period from the commencement of the International Financial Services Centres Authority (Fund Management) regulations for schemes to appoint a custodian in the IFSC.

metadata.contained_chapter: Chapter VIII - General Obligations and Responsibilities

content: Services Centres Authority (Fund Management) (Amendment) Regulation, 2026, during which period the FME may appoint an independent custodian in India, or any foreign jurisdiction, which is regulated by the financial sector regulator in that jurisdiction and make necessary arrangement to provide such
summary: Under Section 132 of the Services Centres Authority (Fund Management) (Amendment) Regulation, 2026, an FME may appoint an independent custodian regulated by a financial sector regulator in India or a foreign jurisdiction.

metadata.contained_chapter: Chapter VIII - General Obligations and Responsibilities

content: and make necessary arrangement to provide such information to Authority whenever directed to do so.
summary: Section 132 mandates that the FME must provide information to the Authority whenever directed to do so regarding custodial arrangements.

metadata.contained_chapter: Chapter VIII - General Obligations and Responsibilities

content: A close-ended scheme, unless its tenure is extended as specified under these regulations, shall be fully redeemed at the end of maturity period.
summary: Section 133 requires that a close-ended scheme must be fully redeemed at the end of its maturity period unless the tenure is extended in accordance with the regulations.

metadata.contained_chapter: Chapter VIII - General Obligations and Responsibilities

content: (1) FME shall prepare an annual report of accounts of the schemes and abridged summary thereof, in respect of each financial year and shall submit the same to the Authority not later than four months from the end of financial year
summary: Section 134(1) requires the FME to prepare an annual report and abridged summary of scheme accounts, submitting them to the Authority within four months of the financial year-end.

metadata.contained_chapter: Chapter VIII - General Obligations and Responsibilities

content: (2) The annual report and abridged summary shall contain details that are necessary for the purpose of providing a true and fair view of the operations of the scheme
summary: Section 134(2) mandates that the annual report and abridged summary must contain sufficient details to provide a true and fair view of the scheme's operations.

metadata.contained_chapter: Chapter VIII - General Obligations and Responsibilities

content: (3) An abridged summary of the annual report of the scheme shall be shared with the investors within four months from the end of the financial year: Provided that if an investor seeks the full annual report, the FME shall provide the same within fifteen (15) days from the date of the receipt of
summary: Section 134(3) requires the FME to share the abridged annual report with investors within four months of the financial year-end and provide the full report within 15 days of an investor's request.

metadata.contained_chapter: Chapter VIII - General Obligations and Responsibilities

content: fifteen (15) days from the date of the receipt of such request.
summary: Section 134(3) specifies that the FME must fulfill an investor's request for a full annual report within fifteen days of receiving said request.

metadata.contained_chapter: Chapter VIII - General Obligations and Responsibilities

content: (1) Every scheme launched by FME shall have the annual statement of accounts audited by an auditor who is not in any way associated with the FME. (2) The auditor shall be appointed by the fiduciaries
summary: Section 135(1) and (2) require that every scheme's annual statement of accounts be audited by an independent auditor appointed by the fiduciaries who is not associated with the FME.

metadata.contained_chapter: Chapter VIII - General Obligations and Responsibilities

content: (3) The auditor shall forward his report to the fiduciaries and such report shall form part of the Annual Report of the schemes.
summary: Section 135(3) mandates that the auditor must submit their report to the fiduciaries, and this report must be included as part of the scheme's Annual Report.

metadata.contained_chapter: Chapter VIII - General Obligations and Responsibilities

content: (1) The FME shall ensure that investors are provided information about their holding in the schemes of FME at the end of every month and within ten working days in case of receipt of such request from an investor
summary: Section 136(1) requires the FME to provide investors with information regarding their scheme holdings at the end of every month or within ten working days upon receiving an investor's request.

metadata.contained_chapter: Chapter VIII - General Obligations and Responsibilities

content: (2) The fiduciaries shall make such disclosures to the investors as are essential in order to keep them informed about any information which may have an adverse bearing on their investments.
summary: Section 136(2) mandates that fiduciaries must disclose all information to investors that could have an adverse impact on their investments.

metadata.contained_chapter: Chapter VIII - General Obligations and Responsibilities

content: The FME shall not undertake any business activities other than as specified under these regulations, without prior approval of the Authority: Provided that a FME operating in the form of branch in an IFSC shall inform the Authority within fifteen (15) days regarding any approval obtained from the
summary: Section 137 prohibits a Fund Management Entity (FME) from conducting business activities outside specified regulations without prior Authority approval, requiring branches in an IFSC to notify the Authority within 15 days of obtaining sectoral regulator approvals.

metadata.contained_chapter: Chapter VIII - General Obligations and Responsibilities

content: days regarding any approval obtained from the sectoral regulator in its principal place of operations, if the activity it intends to conduct outside IFSC requires such specific approval
summary: Section 137 requires FMEs to notify the Authority within 15 days of receiving approval from a sectoral regulator in their principal place of operations for activities conducted outside the IFSC.

metadata.contained_chapter: Chapter VIII - General Obligations and Responsibilities

content: Provided further that FME intending to open a branch or representative office in other jurisdictions for the purpose of marketing their offerings and client service shall give prior intimation to the Authority with the details regarding such branch or representative office.
summary: Section 137 mandates that FMEs provide prior intimation to the Authority with full details before opening branches or representative offices in other jurisdictions for marketing or client service purposes.

metadata.contained_chapter: Chapter VIII - General Obligations and Responsibilities

content: (1) The Authority may suo motu or upon receipt of information or complaint at any time appoint one or more persons as Inspecting Authority to undertake the inspection of the books, accounts, records, documents, infrastructure, procedures, systems of a FME or any other entity associated with the
summary: Section 138(1) empowers the Authority to appoint an Inspecting Authority, either suo motu or upon complaint, to inspect the books, records, infrastructure, and systems of an FME or associated entities.

content: of a FME or any other entity associated with the activities under these regulations for any purpose or to investigate the affairs of schemes and activities as detailed under these regulations including the purposes as specified under sub-regulation (2)
summary: Section 138(1) authorizes the inspection and investigation of an FME's affairs, schemes, and associated activities to ensure compliance with regulatory requirements.

content: (2) The purposes referred to in sub-regulation (1) may include, - (a) to ensure that the books of account, records and documents are being maintained in the manner as required under these regulations; (b) to ensure that the provisions of the Act, the regulations and circulars issued thereunder, are
summary: Section 138(2)(a)-(b) defines inspection purposes as verifying that books of account and records are maintained correctly and ensuring compliance with the Act, regulations, and circulars.

content: regulations and circulars issued thereunder, are complied with; (c) to ascertain whether adequate internal control systems, procedures and safeguards have been established or are being followed by the FME or other entities to fulfil its obligations under these regulations; (d) to ascertain whether
summary: Section 138(2)(c) authorizes inspections to evaluate whether an FME or associated entity has established and followed adequate internal control systems, procedures, and safeguards.

content: under these regulations; (d) to ascertain whether any circumstances exist which would render the FME or other entities unfit or ineligible; (e) to inquire into the complaints received from the investors, clients, other market participants, or any other person on any matter having a bearing on the
summary: Section 138(2)(d)-(e) permits inspections to determine if an FME remains fit and eligible and to investigate complaints from investors, clients, or market participants regarding the FME's activities.

content: person on any matter having a bearing on the activities of the FME; and (f) to inquire suo motu into such matters as may be deemed fit in the interest of investors or the financial market in IFSC
summary: Section 138(2)(f) grants the Authority the power to conduct suo motu inquiries into any matter deemed necessary for the protection of investors or the integrity of the IFSC financial market.

content: (3) Before undertaking an inspection under sub-regulation (1), the inspecting authority shall give a notice to the FME or such other entities referred to in sub- regulations (1): Provided that where the inspecting authority is satisfied that in the interest of the investors no such notice should be
summary: Section 138(3) mandates that the inspecting authority provide notice to the FME before conducting an inspection.

content: of the investors no such notice should be given, it may, for reasons to be recorded in writing, dispense with such notice.
summary: Under Section 138, the inspecting authority may dispense with the notice requirement if it is in the interest of investors, provided the reasons are recorded in writing.

content: (1) Where an inspection of a FME is undertaken by the Authority, such FME and every principal officer, partner, designated partner, trustee, director, chairperson, CEO, KMPs, officer, employee and any agent, etc., of the FME shall provide all assistance and cooperate with the Inspecting Authority
summary: Section 139(1) requires FME personnel, including officers, directors, and employees, to provide full assistance and cooperation to the Inspecting Authority during an inspection.

content: and cooperate with the Inspecting Authority and shall furnish books of accounts, records and documents to the Inspecting Authority with such statements and information relating to its activities within such time as decided by the Inspecting Authority
summary: Section 139(1) mandates that FMEs furnish all requested books of accounts, records, documents, and information to the Inspecting Authority within the specified timeframe.

content: (2) The FME shall give all assistance as may be required in connection with the inspection and allow the Inspecting Authority to have reasonable access to its premises and extend reasonable facility for examining any books of accounts, records and documents in its possession, and also provide
summary: Section 139(2) requires FMEs to grant the Inspecting Authority reasonable access to premises and facilities for the examination of books, records, and documents.

content: and documents in its possession, and also provide copies of records or documents or other material which in the opinion of the inspecting authority are relevant for the purposes of the inspection
summary: Section 139(2) obligates FMEs to provide copies of records, documents, or materials deemed relevant by the Inspecting Authority for inspection purposes.

content: (3) The Inspecting Authority, in the course of inspection, shall be entitled to examine or record the statements of any principal officer, partner, designated partner, trustee, director, chairperson, officer, employee and any agent of the FME (4) It shall be the duty of every principal officer,
summary: Section 139(3) authorizes the Inspecting Authority to examine or record statements from any FME principal officer, partner, director, or employee during an inspection.

content: It shall be the duty of every principal officer, partner, designated partner, trustee, director, chairperson, CEO, KMPs, officer, employee and any agent of the FME to give to the inspecting officer all assistance in connection with the inspection, which the inspecting officer may require.
summary: Section 139(4) establishes the duty of all FME personnel, including KMPs and agents, to provide all necessary assistance required by the inspecting officer.

content: (1) The Authority may appoint an auditor to inspect the books of account, records, documents infrastructures, systems and procedures or affairs of an FME: Provided that the auditor so appointed shall have the same powers of an inspecting authority: Provided further that a FME and its employees
summary: Section 140(1) empowers the Authority to appoint an auditor to inspect FME books, records, and systems, granting such auditor the same powers as an Inspecting Authority.

content: Provided further that a FME and its employees shall have the same obligations towards the auditor as they have towards the Inspecting Authority as specified under regulation 139
summary: Section 140(1) stipulates that FMEs and their employees owe the same obligations to an appointed auditor as they do to the Inspecting Authority under Section 139.

content: (2) The Authority may appoint a valuer to value the scheme assets if considered necessary. (3) The Authority shall be entitled to recover expenses including fees paid to the auditors / valuer as may be incurred by it, for such inspection.
summary: Under Section 140, the Authority is empowered to appoint a valuer for scheme assets and recover all associated inspection expenses, including auditor and valuer fees, from the entity.

content: The Inspecting Authority shall submit the inspection report to the Authority, and the Authority may take such action as it may deem fit and appropriate, on such report: Provided that if directed to do so by the Authority, the Inspecting Authority may submit an interim report.
summary: Section 141 mandates that the Inspecting Authority submit inspection reports, including interim reports if requested, to the Authority for appropriate regulatory action.

content: The Authority may, after consideration of the inspection report and after giving reasonable opportunity of being heard to the FME and/ or its partner, designated partner, trustee, director, chairperson, CEO, KMPs, and any agent, etc., issue such direction as it deems fit in the interest of
summary: Section 142 authorizes the Authority to issue directions following an inspection report, provided the FME, its partners, trustees, directors, or KMPs are granted a reasonable opportunity to be heard.

content: such direction as it deems fit in the interest of financial market or the investors, including directions in the nature of:- (a) requiring FME not to launch new schemes or raise money from investors for a particular period; (b) prohibiting the person concerned from disposing of any of the
summary: Under Section 142, the Authority may issue directions to protect financial markets or investors, including prohibiting an FME from launching new schemes or disposing of assets acquired in violation of regulations.

content: the person concerned from disposing of any of the properties of the fund or scheme acquired in violation of these regulations; (c) requiring the person concerned to dispose of the assets of the fund or scheme in a manner as per the directions; (d) requiring the person concerned to refund any money
summary: Section 142 empowers the Authority to mandate the disposal of assets acquired in violation of regulations and require the refund of money or assets to investors.

content: the person concerned to refund any money or the assets to the concerned investors, along with or without interest, collected under the scheme; (e) prohibiting the person concerned from operating in the financial market or from accessing the financial market for a specified period; (f) any other
summary: Section 142 allows the Authority to order refunds to investors, with or without interest, and prohibit concerned persons from accessing or operating in the financial market for a specified period.

content: market for a specified period; (f) any other direction(s), as the Authority may deem appropriate.
summary: Section 142 grants the Authority broad discretionary power to issue any other directions deemed appropriate in the interest of the financial market or investors.
Section 143 establishes the regulatory framework for enforcement actions against an FME.

The Authority may take such action as deemed fit, including suspension or cancellation of registration, against a FME if it:- (a) fails to exercise due diligence or comply with any conditions subject to which a certificate of registration has been granted; (b) contravenes any of the provisions
summary: Section 143 authorizes the Authority to suspend or cancel an FME's registration for failure to exercise due diligence, non-compliance with registration conditions, or contravention of the Act, rules, or regulations.

metadata.contained_chapter: Chapter X - Liability for action in case of default

summary: Under Section 143, the Authority may take enforcement action against an FME for failing to furnish requested information or providing false information regarding its activities.

metadata.contained_chapter: Chapter X - Liability for action in case of default

content: by the Authority; (d) furnishes to the Authority information which is false or misleading in any material particular; (e) does not submit periodic returns or reports as directed by the Authority; (f) does not co-operate in any enquiry, inspection or investigation conducted by the Authority; (g)
summary: Section 143 outlines grounds for regulatory action, including furnishing false or misleading information, failure to submit periodic returns, or non-cooperation with Authority inquiries, inspections, or investigations.

summary: Section 145(1) authorizes the Authority to grant exemptions from regulatory requirements for up to 18 months to facilitate innovation in products, strategies, processes, services, and business models.

content: (3) Notwithstanding such repeal and supersession, — (a) anything done or any action taken or purported to have been done or taken including registration or approval granted, suspended or cancelled, fees collected, any adjudication, enquiry or investigation commenced or show-cause notice issued,
summary: Section 148(3)(a) provides that actions taken, registrations granted, or investigations commenced under repealed regulations or superseded circulars are deemed to have been taken under the corresponding provisions of these regulations.

content: commenced or show-cause notice issued, under the repealed regulations, superseded circulars, shall be deemed to have been done or taken or commenced under the corresponding provisions of these regulations; (b) any application made to the Authority under the repealed regulations, prior to such
summary: Section 148(3)(a)-(b) ensures continuity by deeming pending applications and ongoing legal or administrative processes initiated under repealed regulations as valid under the current regulatory framework.

content: under the repealed regulations, prior to such repeal, and pending before it shall be deemed to have been made under the corresponding provisions of these regulations; (c) the previous operation of the repealed regulations, superseded circulars or anything duly done or suffered thereunder, any
summary: Section 148(3)(b)-(c) establishes that applications pending before the Authority prior to repeal are treated as if made under these regulations, while preserving the legal status of previous operations.

content: or anything duly done or suffered thereunder, any right, privilege, obligation or liability acquired, accrued or incurred under the repealed regulations, any penalty incurred in respect of any violation committed against the repealed regulations, or any investigation, legal proceeding or remedy in
summary: Section 148(3)(c) preserves rights, privileges, obligations, liabilities, and penalties acquired or incurred under the repealed regulations and superseded circulars.

content: any investigation, legal proceeding or remedy in respect of any such right, privilege, obligation, liability, penalty as aforesaid, shall remain unaffected as if the repealed regulations have never been repealed
summary: Section 148(3)(c) mandates that investigations, legal proceedings, and remedies related to past violations or obligations remain unaffected by the repeal, as if the previous regulations remained in force.

content: (4) After the repeal of International Financial Services Centres Authority (Fund Management) Regulations, 2022, any reference thereto in any other regulations made, guidelines or circulars issued thereunder by the Authority, any act of the Government of India or laws enacted by other statutory
summary: Section 148(4) mandates that references to the repealed International Financial Services Centres Authority (Fund Management) Regulations, 2022, in any government acts, laws, or other regulations shall be construed as references to the corresponding provisions of the new regulations.

content: of India or laws enacted by other statutory authorities shall be deemed to have the reference to the corresponding provisions of these regulations
summary: This provision clarifies that legal references to the repealed 2022 regulations within Indian law or statutory authority frameworks are automatically deemed to refer to the current corresponding regulatory provisions.

content: (5) Save as otherwise contained in sub-regulation (2), the circulars or guidelines issued by Authority under the International Financial Services Centres Authority (Fund Management) Regulations, 2022, shall be deemed to have been issued under these regulations unless and until they are specifically
summary: Section 148(5) establishes that circulars and guidelines issued under the repealed 2022 Fund Management Regulations remain in force and are deemed issued under the current regulations, subject to sub-regulation (2).

content: unless and until they are specifically superseded or modified by the Authority.
summary: Existing circulars and guidelines issued by the Authority remain valid and effective unless explicitly superseded or modified by the Authority.

content: FIRST SCHEDULE (Regulation 3) MANNER OF APPLICATION FOR REGISTRATION Applicants shall file their application in the SWIT and shall provide the following declarations and undertakings as part of their application in the SWIT: a) We hereby declare that the information supplied in the application,
summary: Regulation 3 of the First Schedule requires applicants to submit registration applications via the SWIT platform, including mandatory declarations and undertakings regarding the accuracy of the provided information.

content: that the information supplied in the application, including the attachments thereto, is complete and true
summary: Applicants must formally declare that all information and attachments submitted in their registration application are complete and true.

content: b) The activities proposed in the IFSC are in line with the object clause. c) The applicant and its principal officers, directors/ partners/ designated partners, key managerial personnel and controlling shareholders are fit and proper persons
summary: Applicants must certify that proposed IFSC activities align with their object clause and that all key personnel, including directors, partners, and controlling shareholders, meet the 'fit and proper' criteria.

content: d) We shall ringfence the operations of branch from other operations of the applicant (applicable in case of branch) e) We shall notify IFSCA immediately of any material change in the information provided in the application
summary: Applicants must commit to ringfencing branch operations from other business activities and provide an undertaking to notify the IFSCA immediately of any material changes to application information.

content: f) We shall ensure that the key activities of Investment decision, portfolio management and grievance handling shall be undertaken from IFSC. g) We shall ensure that Key Management Personnel as required under regulation 7 shall be based out of IFSC
summary: Applicants must ensure that core functions, including investment decisions, portfolio management, and grievance handling, are performed from the IFSC, with required Key Management Personnel based locally.

content: h) We further undertake to comply with and be bound by the International Financial Services Centres Authority Act, 2019, and the regulations, circulars, guidelines and instructions thereunder as issued by IFSCA from time to time
summary: Applicants must formally undertake to comply with the International Financial Services Centres Authority Act, 2019, and all subsequent regulations, circulars, and guidelines issued by the IFSCA.

content: i) We further agree that as a condition of registration, we shall at all times abide by such operational instructions/directives as may be issued by the IFSCA from time to time. j) We shall, to the satisfaction of IFSCA, furnish any other information as may be sought by IFSCA.
summary: Registrants must comply with all operational instructions and directives issued by the IFSCA and furnish any requested information to the satisfaction of the authority.
