content: PART A: CODE OF CONDUCT AND OBLIGATIONS OF THE FUND MANAGEMENT ENTITY (a) The FME shall take all reasonable steps and exercise due diligence to ensure that the investment of funds pertaining to any scheme is not contrary to the provisions of these regulations
summary: The FME must exercise due diligence to ensure all scheme investments strictly adhere to the provisions of the regulations.

content: (b) The FME shall be responsible for the acts of commission or omission by its employees or the persons whose services have been procured by the FME
summary: The FME bears full responsibility for all acts of commission or omission committed by its employees or any third-party service providers.

content: (c) Notwithstanding anything contained in any contract or agreement or termination, the FME or its directors or partners or other officers shall not be absolved of liability to the scheme or its investors for their acts of commission or omission, while holding such position or office
summary: Directors, partners, and officers of the FME remain liable to the scheme and its investors for acts of commission or omission during their tenure, regardless of any contractual termination or agreement to the contrary.

content: (d) The Chief Executive Officer (whatever be the designation) of the FME shall ensure that the FME complies with all the provisions of these regulations and the guidelines or circulars issued in relation thereto from time to time and that the investments made by the FME are in the interest of the
summary: The Chief Executive Officer of the FME is responsible for ensuring regulatory compliance with all guidelines and circulars and ensuring investments align with the interests of unit holders.

content: made by the FME are in the interest of the unit holders and shall also be responsible for the overall risk management function of the FME
summary: The Chief Executive Officer of the FME is explicitly responsible for the overall risk management function of the entity.

content: (e) The FME shall not appoint any person as key personnel who has been found guilty of any economic offence or involved in violation of securities laws
summary: The FME is prohibited from appointing any key personnel who have been convicted of economic offenses or violations of securities laws.

content: (f) The FME shall compute and carry out valuation of investments made by its scheme(s) in accordance with the investment valuation norms specified in Sixth Schedule, and shall publish the same
summary: The FME must compute and publish the valuation of scheme investments in strict accordance with the norms specified in the Sixth Schedule.

content: (g) The FME and its controlling shareholders shall be liable to compensate the affected investors and/or the scheme for any unfair treatment to any investor as a result of inappropriate valuation
summary: The FME and its controlling shareholders are liable to compensate investors or the scheme for any unfair treatment resulting from inappropriate investment valuation.

content: (h) The FME must ensure that all investors are provided with adequate, accurate, explicit and timely information fairly presented in a simple language about the investment policies, investment objectives, financial position and general affairs of the scheme
summary: The FME must provide investors with adequate, accurate, and timely information regarding investment policies, objectives, financial position, and general affairs of the scheme in simple language.

content: (i) The FME shall ensure that the assets and liabilities of each scheme are segregated and ring-fenced from other schemes of the FME; and bank accounts and securities accounts of each scheme are segregated and ring-fenced
summary: The FME is required to segregate and ring-fence the assets, liabilities, bank accounts, and securities accounts of each scheme from other schemes.

content: (j) The FME must not use any unethical means to sell, market or induce any investor to buy their schemes. (k) The FME shall maintain high standards of integrity and fairness in all their dealings and in the conduct of their business
summary: The FME is prohibited from using unethical marketing practices and must maintain high standards of integrity and fairness in all business dealings.

content: (l) The FME shall render at all times high standards of service, exercise due diligence, ensure proper care and exercise independent professional judgment
summary: The FME must provide high-quality services, exercise due diligence, ensure proper care, and maintain independent professional judgment.

content: (m) The FME shall not make any exaggerated statement, whether oral or written, either about their qualifications or capability to render investment management services or their achievements
summary: The FME is prohibited from making exaggerated oral or written statements regarding their qualifications, capabilities, or achievements in providing investment management services.

content: (n) The FME shall ensure compliance with the Anti-Money Laundering/ Combating the Financing of Terrorism (AML/CFT) norms as applicable.
summary: The FME must ensure full compliance with all applicable Anti-Money Laundering (AML) and Combating the Financing of Terrorism (CFT) norms.

content: PART B: CODE OF CONDUCT AND OBLIGATIONS OF FIDUCIARIES (DIRECTORS / DESIGNATED PARTNERS / TRUSTEES OF THE FUND) (a) Based on the legal structure of the fund/scheme, the Board of Directors in case of Company, Designated Partners in case of LLP and Trustees (including the Board in case of a Trustee
summary: Part B defines the code of conduct and obligations for fiduciaries, including Boards of Directors, Designated Partners, and Trustees, based on the legal structure of the fund.

content: (including the Board in case of a Trustee company) in case of a Trust, shall: (i) ensure that the monies of the schemes are invested to achieve the objectives of the scheme and in the interest of the investors
summary: Fiduciaries must ensure that scheme monies are invested to achieve the stated objectives of the scheme and prioritize the interests of the investors.

content: (ii) the assets and liabilities of each scheme are segregated and ring-fenced from other schemes of the FME; and bank accounts and securities accounts of each scheme are segregated and ring-fenced
summary: Fiduciaries must ensure that assets, liabilities, bank accounts, and securities accounts are strictly segregated and ring-fenced for each individual scheme.

content: (iii) ensure that different activities of FME are carried at arm’s length and interest of investor under one activity are not being compromised with those of any other scheme or of other activities of the FME. (iv) have a right to obtain from the FME such information as is considered necessary
summary: Fiduciaries must ensure activities are conducted at arm's length to prevent conflicts of interest between schemes and possess the right to obtain necessary information from the FME.

content: (v) render at all times high standards of service, exercise due diligence, ensure proper care and exercise independent professional judgment. (vi) obtain internal audit reports from independent auditors as and when deemed appropriate
summary: The FME must maintain high service standards, exercise due diligence, and apply independent professional judgment while obtaining independent audit reports as necessary.

content: (vii) hold frequent meetings to ensure it discharges the various responsibilities under these regulations. (viii) communicate in writing to the FME of the deficiencies and checking on the rectification of deficiencies
summary: The FME is required to hold frequent meetings to discharge regulatory responsibilities and must communicate identified deficiencies to the FME in writing while monitoring their rectification.

content: (ix) ensure before the launch of any scheme that it has,— (a) systems in place for its back office, dealing room and accounting; (b) appointed all key personnel; (c) appointed auditors to audit its accounts; (d) designated a compliance officer who shall be responsible for monitoring the compliance
summary: Prior to launching any scheme, the FME must establish back-office, dealing, and accounting systems, appoint key personnel and auditors, and designate a compliance officer.

content: be responsible for monitoring the compliance of the Act, rules and regulations, notifications, guidelines, instructions, etc., issued by the Authority or the Central Government and for redressal of investors grievances; (e) appointed fund administrators registered with the Authority or capabilities
summary: The compliance officer is responsible for monitoring adherence to the Act, regulations, and guidelines, redressing investor grievances, and ensuring the appointment of registered fund administrators.

content: registered with the Authority or capabilities to undertake such activities in-house by the FME (f) obtained, wherever required under these regulations, prior in principle approval from the recognised stock exchange(s) where units are proposed to be listed
summary: The FME must possess in-house capabilities or registered fund administrators and obtain prior in-principle approval from recognized stock exchanges for listing units where required.

content: (x) ensure that FME has not given any undue or unfair advantage to any associates or dealt with any of the associates of the FME in any manner detrimental to interest of the investors. (xi) quarterly review all transactions carried out between the schemes, FME and its associates
summary: The FME must prevent undue advantages to associates, avoid actions detrimental to investors, and conduct quarterly reviews of all transactions between schemes, the FME, and its associates.

content: (xii) shall on a yearly basis review the net-worth of the FME to ensure compliance with the threshold provided in Second Schedule on a continuous basis
summary: The FME is mandated to conduct an annual review of its net-worth to ensure continuous compliance with the thresholds specified in the Second Schedule.

content: (xiii) shall ensure that the scheme/fund property is properly protected, held and administered by proper persons and by a proper number of such persons.
summary: The FME must ensure that scheme or fund property is adequately protected, held, and administered by appropriate persons in sufficient numbers.

content: PART C: CODE OF CONDUCT AND OBLIGATIONS OF PRINCIPAL OFFICER, FUND MANAGER AND COMPLIANCE OFFICER (a) The Principal Officer shall ensure that all the activities of the FME are in accordance with the provisions of these regulations and various circulars and guidelines issued thereunder
summary: The Principal Officer is responsible for ensuring all FME activities comply with the regulations, circulars, and guidelines issued by the Authority.

content: (b) The Principal Officer shall ensure that the funds of the schemes are invested to achieve the objectives of the scheme and in the interest of the investors
summary: The Principal Officer must ensure that scheme funds are invested in alignment with the scheme's stated objectives and the best interests of the investors.

content: (c) Where the Principal Officer has reason to believe that the conduct of business of the FME is not in accordance with these regulations it shall forthwith take such remedial steps as are necessary by it and shall immediately inform the Authority of the violation and the action taken by it
summary: The Principal Officer must take immediate remedial action upon identifying FME business conduct that violates regulations and must notify the Authority of both the violation and the corrective measures taken.

content: (d) The Principal Officer shall periodically review the investor complaints received and shall ensure immediate redressal of the same by the FME
summary: The Principal Officer is mandated to periodically review investor complaints and ensure the FME provides immediate redressal for such grievances.

content: (e) Principal Officer and Fund Managers shall: (i) abide by the Act, Rules, Regulations, Guidelines and Circulars governing the securities market; (ii) strive for highest ethical and professional standards to enhance the reputation of the markets; (iii) act honestly in dealings with other market
summary: Principal Officers and Fund Managers must comply with all securities market laws, regulations, and circulars while maintaining high ethical and professional standards to uphold market reputation.

content: (iii) act honestly in dealings with other market participants; (iv) act fairly and deal with market participants in a consistent and transparent manner; (v) act with integrity, particularly in avoiding questionable practices and behaviour; (vi) not indulge in any unethical business activities or
summary: Principal Officers and Fund Managers are required to act with honesty, fairness, transparency, and integrity in all dealings with market participants while avoiding unethical practices.

content: indulge in any unethical business activities or professional misconduct involving dishonesty, fraud or deceit or commit any act that could damage the reputation of the organisation or the fund management industry; (vii) identify existing or potential conflicts of interest as per their institutions
summary: Principal Officers and Fund Managers must avoid professional misconduct, including fraud or deceit, and are obligated to identify and address existing or potential conflicts of interest according to institutional policies.

content: conflicts of interest as per their institutions policies and address the same; (viii) not carry out any transaction on behalf of a scheme with any counter party who is an associate of the FME / controlling shareholders unless such transaction is carried a
summary: Transactions between a scheme and an associate of the FME or controlling shareholders are prohibited unless specific conditions are met to protect investor interests.

content: out on arm’s length basis after taking into account the interest of investors and; b. in terms of the provisions of these regulations and circulars issued thereunder
summary: Transactions with associates or controlling shareholders must be conducted on an arm's length basis and strictly adhere to the provisions of applicable regulations and circulars.

content: (ix) not offer or accept any inducement in connection with the affairs or business of managing the funds of investors which is likely to conflict with the duties owed to the investors; (x) not receive any gift or entertainment which is not in adherence of the gift and entertainment policy of the
summary: Principal Officers and Fund Managers are prohibited from accepting or offering inducements that conflict with duties owed to investors and must adhere to the FME's gift and entertainment policy.

content: of the gift and entertainment policy of the FME framed in this regard
summary: All gifts and entertainment received by FME personnel must strictly comply with the internal gift and entertainment policy established by the FME.

content: (f) The KMP designated as compliance officer shall (i) be responsible for monitoring the compliance of the Act, rules and regulations, notifications, guidelines, instructions, etc., issued by the Authority and for redressal of investors grievances immediately
summary: The Key Managerial Personnel (KMP) designated as the compliance officer is responsible for monitoring adherence to all laws, regulations, and Authority guidelines, and for the immediate redressal of investor grievances.

content: (ii) independently report to the Authority any non-compliance observed by him.
summary: The individual is mandated to independently report any observed non-compliance directly to the Authority.

content: PART D: CONFLICT OF INTEREST (a) The FME shall act in a fiduciary capacity towards its investors and shall disclose to the investors, all of interests as and when they arise or seem likely to arise
summary: The Fund Management Entity (FME) must act in a fiduciary capacity toward investors and provide full disclosure of all actual or potential conflicts of interest as they arise.

content: (b) FME shall establish and implement written policies and procedures to identify, monitor and appropriately mitigate conflicts of interest throughout the scope of business
summary: The FME is required to establish and implement written policies and procedures to identify, monitor, and mitigate conflicts of interest across all business operations.

content: (c) FME shall abide by high level principles on avoidance of conflicts of interest with associated persons, as may be specified by the Authority from time to time
summary: The FME must adhere to high-level principles regarding the avoidance of conflicts of interest with associated persons as prescribed by the Authority.

content: (d) FME shall ensure that different activities of FME are carried at arm’s length and interest of investor under one activity are not being compromised with those of any other scheme or of other activities of the FME.
summary: The FME must maintain arm’s length separation between its various activities to ensure that the interests of investors in one scheme are not compromised by other activities or schemes.

content: PART E: CODE OF CONDUCT AND OBLIGATIONS OF THE FUND MANAGEMENT ENTITY ACTING AS A PORTFOLIO MANAGER (a) A FME acting as a Portfolio Manager shall:- (i) ensure that the money received from the client for an investment purpose is deployed as soon as possible for that purpose and money due and payable
summary: A FME acting as a Portfolio Manager must ensure client funds are deployed for investment purposes promptly and that all amounts due to clients are paid immediately.

content: for that purpose and money due and payable to a client is paid forthwith
summary: The FME must ensure that all money due and payable to a client is paid forthwith.

content: (ii) not execute any trade against the interest of the clients in its proprietary account. (iii) obtain in writing, interest of the client in various corporate bodies which enables such client to obtain unpublished price sensitive information of such corporate bodies
summary: A FME acting as a Portfolio Manager is prohibited from executing proprietary trades against client interests and must obtain written disclosure of client interests in corporate bodies that provide access to unpublished price-sensitive information.

content: (iv) where necessary and in the interest of the client, take adequate steps for the transfer of the clients’ securities and for claiming and receiving dividends, interest payments and other rights accruing to the client
summary: The FME must take necessary steps to manage client securities, including facilitating transfers and claiming dividends, interest payments, and other accrued rights.

content: (v) take necessary action for conversion of securities and subscription for renunciation of rights in accordance with the clients’ instruction
summary: The FME is obligated to perform the conversion of securities and subscribe to or renounce rights in strict accordance with the client's instructions.

content: (vi) not use its status as any other registered intermediary to unduly influence the investment decision of the clients while rendering portfolio management services
summary: Portfolio managers are prohibited from leveraging their status as other registered intermediaries to unduly influence client investment decisions.

content: (vii) not make any statement or indulge in any act, practice or unfair competition, which is likely to be harmful to the interests of other portfolio managers or is likely to place such other portfolio managers in a disadvantageous position in relation to the portfolio manager himself, while
summary: Portfolio managers must refrain from unfair competition or practices that harm or disadvantage other portfolio managers.

content: relation to the portfolio manager himself, while competing for or executing any assignment
summary: The prohibition against unfair competition applies specifically when a portfolio manager is competing for or executing any assignment.

content: (viii) ensure that the investors are provided with true and adequate information without making any misguiding or exaggerated claims and are made aware of attendant risks before any investment decision is taken by them; (ix) render the best possible advice to the client having regard to the
summary: Portfolio managers must provide investors with accurate, non-misleading information and ensure awareness of investment risks prior to decision-making.

content: advice to the client having regard to the client's needs and the environment, and his own professional skills; and (x) ensure that all professional dealings are effected in a prompt, efficient and cost-effective manner.
summary: Portfolio managers are required to provide advice tailored to client needs and professional skills, ensuring all dealings are prompt, efficient, and cost-effective.

content: PART A: ROLES AND RESPONSIBILITIES OF TRUSTEE (a) The trustee shall hold the Business assets in trust for the benefit of the unit holders in accordance with the trust deed and the requirements specified in these regulations
summary: The trustee is mandated to hold Business assets in trust for unit holders, adhering to the trust deed and applicable regulatory requirements.

content: (b) The trustee shall enter into an investment management agreement with the investment manager on behalf of the Investment Trust
summary: The trustee is responsible for executing an investment management agreement with the investment manager on behalf of the Investment Trust.

content: (c) The trustee shall oversee activities of the investment manager in the interest of the unit holders and ensure that the investment manager complies with the responsibilities and shall obtain compliance certificate from the investment manager on a quarterly basis
summary: The trustee must oversee the investment manager's activities to protect unit holder interests and obtain quarterly compliance certificates.

content: (d) The trustee shall oversee activities of the project manager, where applicable, with respect to compliance with these regulations and the project implementation agreement/ project management agreement and shall obtain compliance certificate from the project manager on a quarterly basis
summary: The trustee must monitor the project manager's compliance with regulations and project agreements, requiring quarterly compliance certification.

content: (e) The trustee shall make distributions and ensure that investment manager makes timely declaration of distributions to the unit holders
summary: The trustee is responsible for executing distributions and ensuring the investment manager declares distributions to unit holders in a timely manner.

content: (f) The trustee shall ensure that subscription amount is kept in a separate bank account with IFSC banking unit in name of the Investment Trust and is only utilized for adjustment against allotment of units or refund of money to the applicants till the time such units are listed
summary: The trustee must maintain subscription amounts in a separate IFSC banking unit account in the Investment Trust's name, restricted to unit allotment adjustments or applicant refunds until listing.

content: (g) The trustee shall ensure that the remuneration of the valuer is not linked to or based on the value of the asset being valued. (h) The trustee shall ensure that the investment manager convenes meetings of the unit holders and oversee the voting by unitholders and declare outcome of the voting
summary: The trustee must ensure valuer remuneration is independent of asset valuation and oversee the investment manager's duties regarding unitholder meetings, voting processes, and outcome declarations.

content: (i) In case of any change in investment manager due to removal or otherwise,- (i) prior to such change, the trustee shall obtain approval from unit holders and approval from the Authority: Provided that the approval from the Authority shall not be required in respect of an Investment Trust that is
summary: Changing an investment manager requires prior approval from unitholders and the Authority, except for privately placed Investment Trusts whose units are not listed on a recognized stock exchange.

content: in respect of an Investment Trust that is privately placed whose units are not listed on any recognised stock exchange
summary: Privately placed Investment Trusts with unlisted units are exempt from the requirement to obtain Authority approval when changing an investment manager.

content: (ii) the trustee shall appoint the new investment manager within three months from the date of termination of the earlier investment management agreement; (iii) the previous investment manager shall continue to act as such at the discretion of trustee till such time as new investment manager is
summary: Upon termination of an investment management agreement, the trustee must appoint a successor within three months, during which the previous manager may continue at the trustee's discretion.

content: till such time as new investment manager is appointed; (iv) the trustee shall ensure that the new investment manager shall stand substituted as a party in all the documents to which the earlier investment manager was a party; (v) the trustee shall ensure that the earlier investment manager
summary: The trustee must ensure the new investment manager is substituted as a party in all existing legal documents previously held by the outgoing manager.

content: shall ensure that the earlier investment manager continues to be liable for all its acts of omissions and commissions notwithstanding such termination
summary: The outgoing investment manager remains legally liable for all acts, omissions, and commissions occurring during their tenure despite the termination of their agreement.

content: (j) In case of any change in the project manager due to removal or otherwise,– (i) the trustee shall appoint the new project manager within three months from the date of termination of the earlier project implementation agreement/ project management agreement; (ii) the trustee may, either suo motu
summary: Upon removal or change of a project manager, the trustee must appoint a successor within three months of the termination of the project implementation or management agreement.

content: agreement; (ii) the trustee may, either suo motu or based on the advice of the concessioning authority appoint an administrator in connection with an infrastructure project(s); (iii) the previous project manager shall continue to act as such at the discretion of trustee till such time as new
summary: The trustee may appoint an administrator for infrastructure projects, either suo motu or upon advice from the concessioning authority, while the previous project manager continues at the trustee's discretion.

content: the discretion of trustee till such time as new project manager is appointed; (iv) all costs and expenses in this regard will be borne by the new project manager; (v) the trustee shall ensure that the new project manager shall stand substituted as a party in all the documents to which the earlier
summary: The new project manager must bear all costs associated with the transition and be substituted as a party in all documents previously held by the outgoing project manager.

content: a party in all the documents to which the earlier project manager was a party; (vi) the trustee shall ensure that the earlier project manager continues to be liable for all its acts of omissions and commissions for the period during which it served as the project manager, notwithstanding such
summary: The trustee must ensure that a terminated project manager remains liable for all acts, omissions, and commissions occurring during their tenure as project manager.

content: as the project manager, notwithstanding such termination
summary: This clause confirms the continuing liability of a project manager for their actions during their term, regardless of the termination of their appointment.

content: (k) In case of change in control of the project manager in a PPP project, the trustee shall ensure that written consent of the concessioning authority is obtained in terms of the concession agreement prior to such change, where applicable
summary: In the event of a change in control of a project manager for a PPP project, the trustee must obtain written consent from the concessioning authority as required by the concession agreement.

content: (l) The trustee of an Investment Trust shall not invest in units of the Investment Trust in which it is designated as the trustee.
summary: The trustee of an Investment Trust is strictly prohibited from investing in the units of the specific Investment Trust for which they serve as trustee.

content: PART B: ROLES AND RESPONSIBILITIES OF INVESTMENT MANAGER (a) The investment manager shall make the investment decisions with respect to the underlying assets or projects of the Investment Trust including any further investment or divestment of the assets
summary: The investment manager is responsible for all investment decisions regarding the Investment Trust's underlying assets, including executing further investments or divestments.

content: (b) The investment manager shall oversee activities of the project manager with respect to compliance with these regulations and the project implementation agreement/ project management agreement and shall obtain compliance certificate from the project manager on a quarterly basis
summary: The investment manager must oversee the project manager's compliance with regulations and project agreements, requiring the submission of a quarterly compliance certificate.

content: (c) The investment manager shall ensure that the assets of Investment Trust or holdco or SPV have proper legal and marketable titles, to the extent applicable, and that all the material contracts entered into on behalf of Investment Trust or SPV are legal, valid, binding and enforceable by and on
summary: The investment manager must verify that the Investment Trust, holdco, or SPV holds proper legal and marketable titles to assets and that all material contracts are legally binding and enforceable.

content: legal, valid, binding and enforceable by and on behalf of the Investment Trust or SPV
summary: This provision mandates that all material contracts entered into by or on behalf of the Investment Trust or SPV must be legal, valid, binding, and enforceable.

content: (d) The investment manager shall ensure that the investments made by the Investment Trust are in accordance with the investment conditions and in accordance with the investment strategy of the Investment Trust
summary: The investment manager is obligated to ensure that all investments made by the Investment Trust strictly adhere to established investment conditions and the defined investment strategy.

content: (e) The investment manager, in consultation with trustee, shall appoint an auditor for a period of not more than five consecutive years: Provided that the auditor, not being an individual, may be reappointed for a period of another five consecutive years, subject to approval of unitholders in the
summary: The investment manager, in consultation with the trustee, shall appoint an auditor for a maximum five-year term, with potential reappointment for an additional five years subject to unitholder approval.

content: years, subject to approval of unitholders in the annual meeting
summary: Terms of appointment or duration are subject to approval by unitholders during the annual meeting.

content: (f) The investment manager shall ensure that the assets of Investment Trust are adequately insured
summary: The investment manager is mandated to ensure that all assets held by the Investment Trust are adequately insured.

content: (g) The investment manager shall ensure that it has adequate infrastructure and sufficient key personnel with adequate experience and qualification to undertake management of the Investment Trust at all times
summary: The investment manager must maintain adequate infrastructure and employ key personnel with sufficient experience and qualifications to manage the Investment Trust at all times.

content: (h) The investment manager and the investment banker(s) shall be responsible for all activities pertaining to issue and listing of units of the Investment Trust
summary: The investment manager and investment banker(s) are jointly responsible for all activities related to the issuance and listing of Investment Trust units.

content: (i) The investment manager shall be responsible for all activities pertaining to the issue of units of Investment Trust in respect of privately placed Investment Trust whose units are not listed on a recognised stock exchange
summary: The investment manager holds responsibility for all activities concerning the issuance of units for privately placed Investment Trusts that are not listed on a recognized stock exchange.

content: (j) The investment manager and the investment bankers(s) shall ensure that disclosures made in the offer document or placement memorandum contains material, true, correct and adequate disclosures and are in accordance with the requirements specified by Authority or the recognised stock exchange(s),
summary: The investment manager and investment banker(s) must ensure that offer documents or placement memoranda contain material, true, correct, and adequate disclosures compliant with Authority or stock exchange requirements.

content: by Authority or the recognised stock exchange(s), in respect of Investment Trust listed on a recognised stock exchange
summary: Compliance with disclosure requirements set by the Authority or recognized stock exchange(s) applies to Investment Trusts listed on a recognized stock exchange.

content: (k) The investment manager shall ensure that disclosures made in the placement memorandum contains material, true, correct and adequate disclosures and are in accordance with the requirements specified by Authority in respect of privately placed Investment Trust whose units are not listed on a
summary: The investment manager must ensure that placement memoranda for unlisted, privately placed Investment Trusts contain material, true, correct, and adequate disclosures as specified by the Authority.

content: Investment Trust whose units are not listed on a recognised stock exchange
summary: This provision pertains to Investment Trusts whose units are not listed on a recognized stock exchange.

content: (l) The investment manager shall declare distributions to the unit holders in accordance with the distribution policy as provided in these regulations
summary: The investment manager is required to declare distributions to unitholders in strict accordance with the distribution policy defined in the regulations.

content: (m) The Investment manager shall ensure that adequate controls are in place to ensure segregation of its activity as manager of the Investment Trust from its other activities
summary: The Investment Manager must implement adequate controls to ensure the operational segregation of its Investment Trust management activities from its other business activities.

content: (n) The investment manager shall submit to the trustee, - (i) quarterly reports on the activities of the Investment Trust; (ii) valuation reports within fifteen days of the receipt of the valuation report from the valuer; (o) The investment manager shall ensure that the audit of accounts of the
summary: The Investment Manager is required to submit quarterly activity reports to the trustee and provide valuation reports within fifteen days of receipt from the valuer.

content: shall ensure that the audit of accounts of the Investment Trust by the auditor is done not less than once in a year and such report is: (i) submitted to the recognised stock exchange(s) within sixty (60) days of end of such financial year ending March 31st, in respect of Investment Trust listed on
summary: The Investment Manager must ensure an annual audit of the Investment Trust accounts, with reports submitted to recognized stock exchanges within 60 days of the March 31st financial year-end for listed trusts.

content: 31st, in respect of Investment Trust listed on a recognised stock exchange; or (ii) submitted to the trustee and unitholders, either electronically or through physical copies, in respect of privately placed Investment Trust whose units are not listed on a recognised stock exchange
summary: For privately placed Investment Trusts not listed on a stock exchange, the annual audit report must be submitted to the trustee and unitholders via electronic or physical copies.

content: (p) The investment manager may appoint a custodian in order to provide such custodial services as may be authorised by the trustees
summary: The Investment Manager is authorized to appoint a custodian to provide custodial services as approved by the trustees.

content: (q) The investment manager shall place, before its board of directors in the case of a company or the governing board in case of an LLP, a report on activity and performance of the Investment Trust every three months.
summary: The Investment Manager must present a quarterly report on the activity and performance of the Investment Trust to its board of directors or governing board.

content: PART C: ROLES AND RESPONSIBILITIES OF PROJECT MANAGER (a) The project manager shall undertake operations and management of the InvIT assets including making arrangements for the appropriate maintenance, as may be applicable, either directly or through the appointment and supervision of appropriate
summary: The Project Manager is responsible for the operations, management, and maintenance of InvIT assets, either directly or through the supervision of appointed agents.

content: the appointment and supervision of appropriate agents and as required under any project agreement including a concession agreement in the case of a PPP project
summary: The Project Manager must fulfill all operational obligations required under project agreements, including concession agreements for Public-Private Partnership (PPP) projects.

content: (b) If the InvIT invests in under construction projects, the project manager shall,– (i) undertake the operations and management of the projects, either directly or through appropriate agents; (ii) oversee the progress of development, approval status and other aspects of the project up to its
summary: For under-construction projects, the Project Manager must manage operations and oversee development progress, approval status, and execution aspects.

content: status and other aspects of the project up to its completion, in case of appointment of agents for the purpose of execution
summary: The Project Manager is responsible for overseeing project development and completion status, including the supervision of any agents appointed for project execution.

content: (c) The project manager shall discharge all obligations in respect of achieving timely completion of the project implementation agreement/ infrastructure project, wherever applicable, implementation, operation, maintenance and management of such infrastructure project in terms of the project
summary: The project manager is obligated to ensure timely completion, implementation, operation, maintenance, and management of the infrastructure project as defined in the project implementation agreement.

content: infrastructure project in terms of the project management agreement.
summary: The project manager's duties regarding the infrastructure project are governed by the terms specified in the project management agreement.

content: PART D: ROLES AND RESPONSIBILITIES OF SPONSOR (a) The sponsor(s) and sponsor group(s) shall set up the Investment Trust and appoint the trustee(s) of the Investment Trust
summary: Sponsors and sponsor groups are responsible for establishing the Investment Trust and appointing its trustees.

content: (b) The sponsor(s) and sponsor group(s) shall transfer or undertake to transfer to the Investment Trust, its entire shareholding or interest and rights in the holdco and/ or SPV or ownership of the real estate assets or infrastructure projects, as the case may be, subject to a binding agreement and
summary: Sponsors must transfer their entire shareholding, interest, and rights in the holdco, SPV, or real estate/infrastructure assets to the Investment Trust under a binding agreement.

content: case may be, subject to a binding agreement and adequate disclosures in the offer document or placement memorandum, prior to allotment of units of the Investment Trust: Provided that this shall not apply to the extent of any mandatory holding of shares or interest and rights in the holdco and/ or
summary: Asset transfers to the Investment Trust require adequate disclosures in the offer document or placement memorandum prior to unit allotment, excluding mandatory holdings required by law.

content: or interest and rights in the holdco and/ or SPV by the sponsor(s) and sponsor group(s) as per any Act or regulations or circulars or guidelines of government or any regulatory authority or concession agreement
summary: Mandatory share or interest holdings in holdcos or SPVs required by government regulations, circulars, or concession agreements are exempt from the general transfer requirements.

content: (c) With respect to holding of units in the Investment Trust, (i) the sponsor(s) and sponsor group(s) together shall hold not less than twenty per cent
summary: Sponsors and sponsor groups are collectively required to hold a minimum of 20% of the total units of the Investment Trust post-initial offer.

content: (20%) of the total units of the Investment Trust after initial offer of units, on a post-issue basis for a period of not less than 3 years from the date of the listing of such units (ii) Any holding by sponsor in Investment Trust, exceeding twenty per cent
summary: The 20% minimum unit holding by sponsors must be maintained for a lock-in period of at least 3 years from the date of listing.

content: (20%) on a post issue basis, shall be held for a period of not less than one year from the date of listing of such units
summary: Any sponsor holdings in the Investment Trust exceeding the 20% post-issue threshold must be held for a minimum period of one year from the listing date.

content: (d) Sponsor(s) would be responsible for all acts, omissions and representations/ covenants of the Investment Trust related to formation of Investment Trust, sale/ transfer of assets/holdco/SPV to the Investment Trust
summary: Sponsors bear liability for all acts, omissions, representations, and covenants concerning the formation of the Investment Trust and the transfer of assets, holdcos, or SPVs.

content: (e) In case of PPP projects where the InvIT is investing in infrastructure assets through SPV(s), in case such acquiring or holding is disallowed by government or under any provisions of the concession agreement or any other such agreement,– (i) the sponsor may continue to maintain such holding at
summary: For PPP projects where an InvIT invests through an SPV, if government regulations or concession agreements prohibit direct InvIT holding, the sponsor may maintain the holding at the SPV level.

content: sponsor may continue to maintain such holding at the SPV level; (ii) the consolidated value of all such holdings at the SPV level and the value of the units of InvIT held by the sponsor shall not be less than the value of fifteen per cent
summary: The consolidated value of the sponsor's SPV holdings and InvIT units must remain at least fifteen percent of the total post-issue units of the InvIT.

content: (15%) of the total units of the InvIT after initial issue of units on a post-issue basis; (iii) such units of the InvIT and shares or interest in the SPV shall be held for a period of not less than three years from the date of the listing of units of the InvIT; (iv) in case such holding of sponsor
summary: Sponsors must hold their InvIT units and SPV interests for a minimum lock-in period of three years from the date of the InvIT's unit listing.

content: the InvIT; (iv) in case such holding of sponsor in the SPV results in the InvIT not having controlling interest and not having more than fifty one per cent
summary: If the sponsor's SPV holding prevents the InvIT from maintaining a controlling interest or at least fifty-one percent shareholding in the SPV, specific compliance obligations are triggered.

content: (51%) shareholding or interest in the SPV, the sponsor shall enter into a binding agreement with the InvIT to ensure that decisions taken by the sponsor including voting with respect to the SPV are in compliance with the requirements in this circular and not against the interest of the InvITs or
summary: In cases of insufficient InvIT control, the sponsor must execute a binding agreement ensuring all voting and decision-making regarding the SPV aligns with circular requirements and protects InvIT unit holder interests.

content: and not against the interest of the InvITs or the unit holders and shall be subject to further guidelines as may be specified by the Authority.
summary: Sponsor actions regarding SPV governance must prioritize the interests of the InvIT and its unit holders, remaining subject to additional guidelines issued by the Authority.

content: PART E: ROLES AND RESPONSIBILITIES OF VALUER (a) The valuer(s) shall ensure that the valuation of the Business assets is impartial, true and fair and is in accordance with these regulations
summary: Valuers are mandated to perform impartial, true, and fair valuations of business assets in strict accordance with established regulations.

content: (b) The valuer(s) shall ensure that it has sufficient and adequate financial, human and other resources to enable it to perform valuations
summary: Valuers must maintain sufficient financial, human, and operational resources to effectively execute their valuation responsibilities.

content: (c) The valuer(s) and any of its employees involved in valuing of the assets of the Investment Trust, shall not,- (i) invest in units of the Investment Trust or in the assets being valued; and (ii) sell the assets or units of Investment Trust held prior to being appointed as the valuer, till the
summary: Valuers and their employees are prohibited from investing in the Investment Trust's units or the specific assets they are tasked with valuing.

content: prior to being appointed as the valuer, till the time such person is designated as valuer of such Investment Trust and not less than six months after ceasing to be valuer of the Investment Trust
summary: Valuers must divest or refrain from selling Investment Trust units or assets held prior to their appointment, continuing through their tenure and for at least six months after ceasing to be the designated valuer.

content: (d) The valuer(s) shall conduct the valuation of the Investment Trust assets with transparency and fairness and shall render, at all times, high standards of service, exercise due diligence, ensure proper care and exercise independent professional judgment
summary: Valuers of Investment Trust assets must maintain transparency, fairness, high service standards, due diligence, and independent professional judgment.

content: (e) The valuer(s) shall act with independence, objectivity and impartiality in performing the valuation
summary: Valuers are mandated to perform all valuation duties with strict independence, objectivity, and impartiality.

content: (f) The valuer(s) shall not accept remuneration, in any form, for performing a valuation of the Investment Trust assets from any person other than the Investment Trust or its authorized representative
summary: Valuers are prohibited from accepting remuneration for Investment Trust asset valuations from any entity other than the Investment Trust or its authorized representative.

content: (g) The valuer(s) shall before accepting any assignment, from any related party to the Investment Trust, shall disclose to the Investment Trust any direct or indirect consideration which the valuer may have in respect of such assignment.
summary: Before accepting assignments from related parties, valuers must disclose any direct or indirect consideration to the Investment Trust.

content: PART F: ROLES AND RESPONSIBILITIES OF AUDITOR (a) The auditor shall conduct audit of the accounts of the Investment Trust and prepare the audit report based on the accounts examined by him and after taking into account the relevant accounting and auditing standards
summary: The auditor is responsible for auditing Investment Trust accounts and preparing reports in accordance with relevant accounting and auditing standards.

content: (b) The auditor shall, to the best of his information and knowledge, ensure that the accounts and financial statements, including profit or loss and cash flow for the period and such other matters as may be specified, give a true and fair view of the state of the affairs
summary: The auditor must ensure that financial statements, including profit, loss, and cash flow, provide a true and fair view of the Investment Trust's affairs.

content: (c) The auditor shall have a right of access at all times to the books of accounts and vouchers pertaining to activities of the Investment Trust
summary: The auditor is granted the right of access to all books of accounts and vouchers related to the Investment Trust's activities at all times.

content: (d) The auditor shall have a right to require such information and explanation pertaining to activities of the Investment Trust as he may consider necessary for the performance of his duties as auditor from the employees of Investment Trust or parties to the Investment Trust or holdco or SPV or any
summary: The auditor has the authority to demand necessary information and explanations from employees, parties to the Investment Trust, holdcos, SPVs, or any person possessing relevant data.

content: to the Investment Trust or holdco or SPV or any other person in possession of such information.
summary: This provision extends the auditor's authority to obtain necessary information and explanations from any person in possession of data relevant to the Investment Trust, holdco, or SPV.

content: FIFTH SCHEDULE ADVERTISEMENT CODE (a) Advertisements shall be accurate, true, fair, clear, complete, unambiguous and concise
summary: The Fifth Schedule Advertisement Code requires all advertisements to be accurate, true, fair, clear, complete, unambiguous, and concise.

content: (b) Advertisements shall not contain statements which are false, misleading, biased or deceptive, based on assumption/projections and shall not contain any testimonials or any ranking based on any criteria
summary: Advertisements are prohibited from containing false, misleading, biased, or deceptive statements, including assumptions, projections, testimonials, or rankings.

content: (c) Advertisements shall not be so designed as likely to be misunderstood or likely to disguise the significance of any statement. Advertisements shall not contain statements which directly or by implication or by omission may mislead the investor
summary: Advertisements must not be designed to be misunderstood, disguise the significance of statements, or mislead investors through direct claims, implications, or omissions.

content: (d) Advertisements shall not carry any slogan that is exaggerated or unwarranted or slogan that is inconsistent with or unrelated to the nature and risk and return profile of the product. (e) Advertisements shall not be so framed as to exploit the lack of experience or knowledge of the investors
summary: Advertisements must avoid exaggerated or inconsistent slogans and are prohibited from exploiting the lack of experience or knowledge of investors.

content: Extensive use of technical or legal terminology or complex language and the inclusion of excessive details which may detract the investors should be avoided. (g) Advertisements shall contain information which is timely and consistent with the disclosures made in the Scheme Documents.
summary: Advertisements must avoid complex legal or technical terminology and excessive detail, ensuring all information is timely and consistent with Scheme Documents.

content:
SIXTH SCHEDULE
INVESTMENT VALUATION NORMS - PRINCIPLES OF FAIR VALUATION
A FME shall value its investments in accordance with the following overarching principles so as to
ensure fair treatment to all investors including existing investors as well as investors seeking to invest:
summary: The Sixth Schedule mandates that a Fund Management Entity (FME) must value investments according to principles of fair valuation to ensure equitable treatment of all investors.

content:
(a) The valuation of investments shall be based on the principles of fair valuation i.e., valuation shall
be reflective of the realizable value of the securities/financial product/assets. The valuation shall be
summary: Investment valuation must reflect the realizable value of securities, financial products, or assets, conducted in good faith through established policies and procedures.

content:
done in good faith and in true and fair manner through appropriate valuation policies and procedures.
(b) The policies and procedures shall identify the methodologies that will be used for valuing each type
of securities/financial products/assets held by the schemes. Investment in new type of
summary: FME valuation policies must define specific methodologies for each asset type, ensuring a true and fair valuation process.

content:
securities/financial products/assets by the scheme shall be made only after establishment of the
valuation methodologies for such securities/ financial products/assets.
(c) The assets held by the FME shall be consistently valued according to the policies and procedures.
summary: Investments in new asset types require prior establishment of valuation methodologies, and all assets must be consistently valued according to approved policies.

content:
The policies and procedures shall describe the process to deal with events where market quotations are
no longer reliable for a particular asset.
(d) The FME shall provide for the periodic review of the valuation policies and procedures to ensure
summary: Valuation policies must include procedures for handling unreliable market quotations and mandate periodic reviews to ensure methodology accuracy and effective implementation.

content:
the appropriateness and accuracy of the methodologies used and its effective implementation in valuing
the securities/financial products/assets.
(e) The valuation policies and procedures approved by the FME should seek to address conflict of
interest.
summary: FME-approved valuation policies must be reviewed periodically for accuracy and specifically designed to address and mitigate conflicts of interest.

content:
interest.
(f) Disclosure of the valuation policy and procedures (with regard to valuation of each category of
securities/financial product/assets where the scheme will invest, situation where these methods will be
summary: Requires disclosure of valuation policies and procedures for all categories of securities, financial products, and assets held by the scheme.

content:
used, process and methodology and impact of implementation of these methods, if any) shall be made
in offer document and on the website of the FME to ensure transparency of valuation norms to be
adopted by FME. This clause shall be applicable only in respect of Retail Schemes.
summary: Mandates that Retail Schemes publish valuation methodologies, processes, and implementation impacts in the offer document and on the FME website to ensure transparency.

content:
(g) The responsibility of true and fairness of valuation and correct NAV shall be of the FME,
irrespective of disclosure of the approved valuation policies and procedures i.e., if the established
summary: Establishes that the FME bears ultimate responsibility for the accuracy, fairness, and correct Net Asset Value (NAV) of the scheme, regardless of disclosed policies.

content:
policies and procedures of valuation do not result in fair/ appropriate valuation, the FME shall deviate
from the established policies and procedures in order to value the assets/ financial products / securities
at fair value:

summary: Authorizes the FME to deviate from established valuation policies and procedures when such policies fail to produce a fair or appropriate valuation of assets.

that any deviation from the disclosed valuation policy and procedures may be allowed with
